description_long: This triumphal arch is a long, rectangular structure featuring a series of evenly spaced arches supported by thick piers. The upper section is solid and flat, forming a continuous entablature above the arches, with no visible decorative reliefs or sculptural embellishments. The arches are tall and narrow, creating a rhythmic pattern along the length of the structure. The material appears to be a dark, stone-like substance, giving the object a robust and monumental appearance. There are no visible inscription panels or ornate details, emphasizing a utilitarian and classical design. The overall form is reminiscent of ancient Roman aqueducts or triumphal arches, with a focus on symmetry and structural clarity.
description: A long, dark stone triumphal arch with multiple tall, narrow arches and a solid upper entablature, lacking decorative details. The design is simple, robust, and classical.
description_view_0: A narrow end view showing the thickness of the structure and the pointed tops of the arches.
description_view_1: A side view displaying the full length of the arch, with all arches and supporting piers visible.
description_view_2: Another narrow end view, nearly identical to view 0, showing the structure's thickness.
description_view_3: A side view similar to view 1, showing the opposite side of the arch with the same series of arches.
